evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Internally consistent wagmi v2 API: createConfig, WagmiProvider, useAccount/useConnect/useDisconnect/useSignMessage/useReadContract with `query.enabled`, injected/walletConnect connectors. Version pins 2.14/2.21 are plausible v2-era. USDC contract on Base Sepolia (0x036C...CF7e) matches Circle's published address.
evidence.executability: All fenced blocks are valid TS/bash. Components correctly marked 'use client'. One minor: code reads USDC on baseSepolia.id but uses mainnet USDC contract referenced for base mainnet would differ — playbook uses Sepolia consistently. No sandbox-infeasible patterns (no ngrok/sudo). Test file uses jest/testing-library which isn't installed in deps — minor fix needed.
evidence.completeness: (a) WalletConnect projectId acquisition URL given; (b) install bash present; (c) env vars (NEXT_PUBLIC_WALLETCONNECT_PROJECT_ID) listed and referenced consistently; (d) no backend (not needed for client-only Web3); (e) frontend code complete; (f) test command (npm run dev) and jest example present. All six pieces extractable.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Citations are mostly third-party blogs (Medium, c-sharpcorner, markaicode). Some semi-official (docs.base.org, docs.metamask.io, docs.cdp.coinbase.com) present. Official wagmi.sh only cited as v1 legacy URL. All citations at end, not anchored near code.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true; long-tail handling applied. Playbook is internally consistent with wagmi v2 API surface and explicit version pins, supporting score 4 on correctness.
